description_long: This is a large, ancient-looking vase with a wide, flared rim and a tall, tapering body. The surface is rough and uneven, showing visible cracks and patchwork repairs, suggesting it has been reconstructed from fragments. The coloration is predominantly earthy brown with subtle variations, giving it a weathered, archaeological appearance. The rim is irregular and slightly chipped, while the body displays a patchwork of darker and lighter tones. There are no visible decorative motifs or embellishments, emphasizing its utilitarian or historical character. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding objects or substances.
description: A tall, weathered brown vase with a flared, chipped rim and visible cracks, giving it an ancient, reconstructed look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, uneven rim and the most prominent cracks and repairs on the upper body.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the vase's tapering form and a smoother section of the surface with fewer visible cracks.
description_view_2: The view reveals the full curvature of the vase and a more continuous, less fragmented surface on one side.
description_view_3: This side displays deeper color variations and a concentration of cracks near the rim and upper body.
